cf_parsed.subtask_body:
Rechecked whether another turn is worth it from the same prior state.
Graph coloring remains very strong: the proposed recolors eliminate the 9 listed baseline conflicts, and no replacement conflict is apparent on any recolored node neighborhood; zero-conflict cost 4 is optimal if valid.
Steiner also still looks settled: any tree spanning Bay, Pine, Mill with only those 3 terminals plus optional Steiner nodes needs at least 3 edges. The cheapest 3-edge terminal-spanning trees I can form are cost 33, 35, or more; the 33-edge option Bay-Pine, Pine-Port, Port-Mill activates {Bay,Pine,Port,Mill} and forces 3 frequencies because Bay-Port and Pine-Port both interfere while Bay-Pine does not, so total 78. The 35-edge baseline tree needs only 2 frequencies, total 65, and alternatives at edge cost 35 or 36 also seem to require 2+ frequencies, so 65 is very likely optimal.
For TSP, I did one more geometric sanity pass. The current tour is valid and plausibly around the mid-450s, but I do not have a confidently better replacement without risking a worse tour. Since graph coloring and steiner likely already capture full value, only limited upside remains.
Thus the safest portfolio answer is unchanged from current best.
cf_parsed.updated_plan_state: Keep prior plan effectively unchanged: steiner likely optimal at 65, graph coloring likely optimal at zero conflicts, and only TSP offers residual upside; however, absent a clearly superior tour, preserve the current valid portfolio answer.

transcript.content:
PLAN_STATE: Prioritize exact-by-reasoning improvements on steiner and graph coloring, then use remaining effort on a clearly better TSP tour than baseline.
Steiner likely has a small state space; enumerate promising terminal-connecting trees and minimize frequencies jointly.
Graph coloring: repair baseline conflicts while preserving proper colors on dense neighborhoods; aim for zero conflicts since graph is stated 4-colorable.
TSP: exploit Euclidean geometry with cluster ordering and crossing elimination; target a tour materially below 494.8.
Use one main subtask to solve all three by hand reasoning, with most attention to graph coloring and steiner because they may yield near-optimal/optimal gains.
If a clean zero-conflict coloring is found, stop unless TSP still looks weak.
If steiner optimum is certified and graph coloring is conflict-free, any extra turn only worthwhile for TSP local improvements.
Need final output as single JSON object keyed by all three ids, schemas exact.
NEXT_SUB: {"id":1,"desc":"Derive a low-cost Steiner tree with feasible frequencies, construct a conflict-free 4-coloring, and produce an improved Euclidean TSP tour via geometric ordering","p_solve":0.72,"time_budget_s":600}

SUB_1: Steiner: best tree I found is Bay-Pine(15), Pine-Cape(4), Cliff-Mill(10) is disconnected, so invalid. Better connected 3-edge trees include Bay-Pine, Pine-Port, Port-Mill = 33 edge cost but Bay/Port and Pine/Port force 3 frequencies on active {Bay,Pine,Port,Mill}, total 78. Bay-Cliff, Cliff-Mill, Bay-Pine baseline is 35+30=65. Best valid connected option found is Bay-Cliff(10), Cliff-Mill(10), Bay-Pine(15), needing 2 frequencies, so 65 likely optimal.
Graph coloring: repaired baseline to zero conflicts by sequential recolors:
N16:1->3 removes N01-N16.
N27:3->2 removes N03-N27 and N23-N27.
N28:4->1 removes N04-N28.
N30:4->2 removes N08-N30.
N26:4->1 removes N08-N26.
N15:3->1 removes N10-N15.
N29:4->3 removes N12-N29.
Then all checked incident constraints remain proper; cost 4.
TSP: constructed a perimeter-style tour by geometric sweep and checked rough edge lengths. Candidate
[7,9,17,12,10,15,19,1,0,2,5,18,8,13,14,16,6,11,3,4]
has approximate total around 457, clearly beating baseline 494.8.
